title: gentiana polymorpha subsp. rhaetica
intro: Gentiana polymorpha subsp. rhaetica, also known as Alpine gentian, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Gentianaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, where it can be found growing in alpine meadows and rocky areas. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The herbal preparations of Gentiana polymorpha subsp. rhaetica, such as teas and tinctures, have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and as a natural remedy to boost the immune system.
